findings: Design constraint confirmed: all styles must be inline CSS — no CDN, no external fonts, no runtime dependencies; HTML is generated as a Node.js template string in keel-dashboard.cjs.
Font stack chosen: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif — zero network requests, renders as SF Pro / Segoe UI / system default per OS.
Color palette finalized: background #f8fafc, surface #ffffff, table header #1e293b, text #0f172a, muted #64748b, border #e2e8f0.
Status badge colors adopted verbatim from cmdReport / conventions.md: COMPLETE=#16a34a (green), IN PROGRESS=#d97706 (amber), HALTED=#dc2626 (red). No new color variants introduced.
Badge shape: pill (border-radius: 9999px), 12px bold white text, padding 3px 10px, white-space: nowrap — prevents wrapping on narrow Status column.
Table columns in order: Story ID (100px bold), Title (auto-grow), Scope (90px muted), Current Phase (160px, shows 'Phase N — Agent Name'), Status (130px badge), Idle Time (100px right-aligned).
Auto-refresh implemented via <meta http-equiv='refresh' content='30'> in <head>; visible indicator is an animated pulse dot (CSS @keyframes pulse) labeled 'Live · refreshes every 30s' in header.
The @keyframes pulse animation is the only <style> block in the document — required because keyframe animations cannot be expressed as inline style attributes.
Empty state: rendered instead of the table when no stories exist; centered div with 📂 icon (48px), 'No stories found.' heading, and 'keel init <story-id>' in a code span with #e2e8f0 background.
Error row pattern: malformed manifest renders with #fef2f2 (light red) row background, story_id in red italic, error message prefixed with ⚠ in the Title column, em-dashes in remaining columns.
Layout: max-width 960px centered container, overflow-x: auto table wrapper for narrow viewports, header/main/footer flex column filling the viewport height.
Footer: two-column flex row (justify-content: space-between) showing 'Last updated: <ISO timestamp>' left and 'Port <N>' right — directly addresses AC-5 port visibility.
Table header row uses dark background #1e293b with #f1f5f9 text, 12px uppercase 0.05em letter-spacing — matches developer CLI aesthetic without any external framework.
Row hover state implemented via onmouseover/onmouseout inline handlers setting background to #f1f5f9 — no JavaScript framework required; alternating row colors #ffffff / #f8fafc for odd/even.

Mockup file docs/design/KEEL-104-dashboard-mockup.html is fully self-contained (opens as file:// URL), uses fake data: KEEL-101 COMPLETE, KEEL-102 IN PROGRESS phase 5, KEEL-103 HALTED, KEEL-104 IN PROGRESS phase 3.
decisions: Inline CSS only (no CDN/Tailwind/Bootstrap): the server generates HTML as a string — external stylesheets would require serving additional routes or bundling, adding complexity that contradicts the zero-dependency constraint.
Single @keyframes pulse <style> block permitted: keyframe animations have no inline-style equivalent; this is the minimal exception to the inline-only rule and has no runtime dependencies.

Status badge colors match cmdReport exactly (not approximate): design consistency between CLI and browser matters more than a 'prettier' palette; engineers should never need to map between two color systems.
Idle Time column right-aligned with white-space: nowrap: numbers right-align naturally for comparison; nowrap prevents '1h 7m' splitting across lines on narrow columns.

Empty state uses Unicode emoji (📂) not an SVG icon: SVG would require additional markup and escaping in the template string; the emoji is a single character, renders at any size, and requires no external asset.

Error rows use #fef2f2 background (not a badge): an error is a data-quality signal on the row, not a status classification — using the same badge component would imply parity with HALTED/COMPLETE/IN PROGRESS, which is incorrect.

Phase column shows 'Phase N — Agent Name' (not just integer): the integer alone is meaningless to a user scanning the dashboard; the agent name anchors context without requiring hover or a separate legend.
